creator: cse.cem@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to Ed Maste from comment #19)
> ... I've learned something new today as a result of this discussion: "-l:"
> can be used to specify a library that doesn't start with lib and/or doesn't
> end with .so.  For example:
> 
>   cc foo.c -L /lib/geom -l:geom_part.so

News to me, too. We could print that? Something like:

  printf("symbol %s (try -l%s%s)", name, starts_with_lib? "" : ":", libname)
